messages.content:
CVE-2020-13133 describes a stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability present in Tufin SecureChange prior to versions R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1. This vulnerability arises when the software fails to properly neutralize user-controllable input before displaying it on a web page, classified under CWE-79. Although exploitation requires administrative privileges to store the payload, it can be triggered by unauthenticated users, posing a significant risk to the integrity and confidentiality of web applications.

The vulnerability has been categorized with a medium severity level and is linked to the MITRE ATT\u0026CK technique T1189, which involves drive-by compromise methods. Affected versions include deployments of Tufin SecureChange prior to R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1, with specific vulnerabilities listed in known software configuration enumerations.

To mitigate this vulnerability, organizations should upgrade to the fixed versions R19.3 HF3 and R20-1 HF1. Additional defense measures may involve applying techniques grouped under IDs M1050, M1051, M1048, M1021, and M1017, focusing on preventing script execution and neutralizing potentially harmful user input to safeguard against exploitation.
